name: Chicken Lo Mein Loo
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Chinese egg noodles (dried, not fresh)
2 oz Raw lean pork, cut into matchsticks
2 oz Raw lean beef, cut into matchbook-sized pieces
2 oz Raw chicken, cut into small pieces of any shape
2 oz Raw shelled shrimp (cooked will do)
2 oz Button mushrooms, sliced (reconstituted Chinese ones are better but quite expensive)
2 cups Chinese cabbage, shredded (can substitute regular cabbage, blanched)
2 Scallions, cut into matchsticks (green and white parts, no roots)
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add the Chinese egg noodles and cook for 3 minutes. Drain and toss with sesame oil and regular oil; set aside.
2. In four separate dishes, mix each meat with half teaspoon sherry and half teaspoon cornstarch.
3. Heat the oil in a skillet or wok over high heat. Add the meats one at a time, stirring briefly between additions: pork, beef, chicken, shrimp. Once the shrimp are added and tossed, include the mushrooms and cabbage. Cook until the cabbage is wilted. Stir in scallions, soy sauce, salt, and MSG.
4. Add the noodles and bean sprouts to the skillet or wok, stirring briefly for 3 minutes.
5. Serve hot on a warm platter.
